Helper functions for ECMA built-in operations, including object and array manipulation routines.

# Purpose
The code provides a set of helper functions for operations related to ECMAScript objects and arrays, as defined in the ECMA-262 specification. These functions are part of a library that implements built-in JavaScript object behaviors, such as `Object.prototype.toString`, `Array.prototype.toLocaleString`, and `Object.keys`. The code is organized into several functions, each handling specific tasks like converting objects to strings, normalizing array indices, and concatenating values to arrays.

Key functions include [`ecma_builtin_helper_object_to_string`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_object_to_string>), which implements the `Object.prototype.toString` routine, and [`ecma_builtin_helper_get_to_locale_string_at_index`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_get_to_locale_string_at_index>), which assists in the `Array.prototype.toLocaleString` operation. The code also includes utility functions like [`ecma_builtin_helper_array_index_normalize`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_array_index_normalize>) and [`ecma_builtin_helper_string_index_normalize`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_string_index_normalize>) for index normalization, and [`ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop>) for defining object properties. These functions are designed to be used internally within the library to ensure compliance with the ECMAScript standard, providing foundational operations for higher-level JavaScript functionality.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ecma-builtin-helpers.h`
- `ecma-alloc.h`
- `ecma-array-object.h`
- `ecma-builtins.h`
- `ecma-conversion.h`
- `ecma-function-object.h`
- `ecma-exceptions.h`
- `ecma-helpers.h`
- `ecma-objects.h`
- `ecma-try-catch-macro.h`
- `lit-magic-strings.h`

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_object\_to\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_object_to_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L51>)

Converts an ECMAScript value to a string representation in the format "[object Type]".
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The ECMAScript value to convert to a string.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `this_arg` is `undefined` and set `type_string` to `LIT_MAGIC_STRING_UNDEFINED_UL` if true.
    - Check if `this_arg` is `null` and set `type_string` to `LIT_MAGIC_STRING_NULL_UL` if true.
    - Convert `this_arg` to an object using [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>) if it is neither `undefined` nor `null`.
    - Return the error value if [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>) results in an error.
    - Assert that the converted value is an object and retrieve the object's class name using [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>).
    - Free the object value after retrieving the class name.
    - Define a buffer to build the string representation in the format "[object Type]".
    - Iterate over predefined magic string identifiers to construct the string in the buffer.
    - Create a new ECMA string from the buffer and return it as an ECMA string value.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the string "[object Type]", where Type is the type of the input value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_free_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_free_value>)
    - [`lit_copy_magic_string_to_buffer`](<../../lit/lit-magic-strings.c.md#lit_copy_magic_string_to_buffer>)
    - [`ecma_new_ecma_string_from_utf8`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_new_ecma_string_from_utf8>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_get\_to\_locale\_string\_at\_index<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_get_to_locale_string_at_index}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L124>)

Retrieves the `toLocaleString` representation of an element at a specified index in an object, handling undefined or null values and ensuring the method is callable.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``obj_p``: A pointer to the `ecma_object_t` object from which to retrieve the element.
    - ``index``: A `uint32_t` representing the index of the element in the object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value and create a string from the `index`.
    - Attempt to get the value at the specified `index` from `obj_p` using [`ecma_op_object_get`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_get>).
    - If the value is `undefined` or `null`, set `ret_value` to an empty string value.
    - Otherwise, convert the value to an object and attempt to retrieve the `toLocaleString` method.
    - Check if the retrieved method is callable; if so, call it and convert the result to a string.
    - If the method is not callable, raise a type error.
    - Release any allocated resources and return `ret_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the `toLocaleString` result of the element at the specified index, or an empty string if the element is `undefined` or `null`, or a type error if the method is not callable.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32>)
    - [`ecma_op_object_get`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_get>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_op_is_callable`](<../operations/ecma-function-object.c.md#ecma_op_is_callable>)
    - [`ecma_op_function_call`](<../operations/ecma-function-object.c.md#ecma_op_function_call>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_string`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_string>)
    - [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_object\_get\_properties<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_object_get_properties}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L196>)

Retrieves the property names of an object and returns them as an array, optionally filtering for only enumerable properties.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``obj_p``: A pointer to the `ecma_object_t` object whose properties are to be retrieved.
    - ``only_enumerable_properties``: A boolean flag indicating whether to include only enumerable properties in the result.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assert that `obj_p` is not NULL.
    - Create a new array object to store the property names.
    - Retrieve the property names of the object using [`ecma_op_object_get_property_names`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_get_property_names>), filtering based on the `only_enumerable_properties` flag.
    - Initialize an iterator for the collection of property names.
    - Iterate over the collection of property names.
    - For each property name, create a string representation of the current index and define the property on the new array object using [`ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop>).
    - Assert that the property definition was successful.
    - Increment the index for the next property.
    - Free the collection of property names after iteration.
    - Return the new array containing the property names.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing an array of the object's property names, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_op_create_array_object`](<../operations/ecma-array-object.c.md#ecma_op_create_array_object>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_op_object_get_property_names`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_get_property_names>)
    - [`ecma_collection_iterator_init`](<../base/ecma-helpers-values-collection.c.md#ecma_collection_iterator_init>)
    - [`ecma_collection_iterator_next`](<../base/ecma-helpers-values-collection.c.md#ecma_collection_iterator_next>)
    - [`ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop>)
    - [`ecma_deref_ecma_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_deref_ecma_string>)
    - [`ecma_free_values_collection`](<../base/ecma-helpers-values-collection.c.md#ecma_free_values_collection>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_array\_index\_normalize<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_array_index_normalize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L262>)

Normalizes an array index to a valid range based on the array's length.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``index``: The index to normalize, which can be a number including special values like NaN or infinity.
    - ``length``: The length of the array, used to determine the valid range for the index.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `index` is not NaN; if it is NaN, set `norm_index` to 0.
    - If `index` is zero, set `norm_index` to 0.
    - If `index` is infinity, set `norm_index` to 0 if negative, or to `length` if positive.
    - If `index` is negative, negate it and check if it exceeds `length`; if it does, set `norm_index` to 0, otherwise set `norm_index` to `length` minus the negated index converted to uint32.
    - If `index` is positive and greater than `length`, set `norm_index` to `length`, otherwise convert `index` to uint32 and set `norm_index`.
- **Output**: Returns the normalized index as a `uint32_t` value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_number_is_zero`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_zero>)
    - [`ecma_number_is_infinity`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_infinity>)
    - [`ecma_number_negate`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_negate>)
    - [`ecma_number_to_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-conversion.c.md#ecma_number_to_uint32>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_array\_concat\_value<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_array_concat_value}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L327>)

Concatenates a given `ecma_value_t` to an array, updating the array's length accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``obj_p``: Pointer to the array object to which the value will be concatenated.
    - ``length_p``: Pointer to the current length of the array, which will be updated.
    - ``value``: The value to concatenate to the array.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Check if `value` is an object and its class name is 'Array'.
    - If true, retrieve the length of the array object `value` and iterate over its elements.
    - For each element, if found, add it to `obj_p` at the appropriate index and update `length_p`.
    - If `value` is not an array, add `value` to `obj_p` at the current `length_p` index and increment `length_p`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, set it to `ECMA_SIMPLE_VALUE_TRUE`.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` indicating success or failure, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_new_ecma_length_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_new_ecma_length_string>)
    - [`ecma_op_object_get`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_get>)
    - [`ecma_number_to_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-conversion.c.md#ecma_number_to_uint32>)
    - [`ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_new_ecma_string_from_uint32>)
    - [`ecma_op_object_find`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_find>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_string\_index\_normalize<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_string_index_normalize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L435>)

Normalizes a string index by clamping it to the range [0, length], handling NaN and negative values according to the `nan_to_zero` parameter.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``index``: The index to normalize, which is a floating-point number.
    - ``length``: The length of the string, which is an unsigned integer.
    - ``nan_to_zero``: A boolean flag indicating whether NaN should be mapped to zero (`true`) or to the length of the string (`false`).
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `norm_index` to 0.
    - Check if `index` is NaN using `ecma_number_is_nan`.
    - If `index` is NaN and `nan_to_zero` is `false`, set `norm_index` to `length`.
    - If `index` is not negative, check if it is infinity using [`ecma_number_is_infinity`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_infinity>).
    - If `index` is infinity, set `norm_index` to `length`.
    - Otherwise, convert `index` to an unsigned integer using [`ecma_number_to_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-conversion.c.md#ecma_number_to_uint32>).
    - If the converted `norm_index` is greater than `length`, set `norm_index` to `length`.
    - Return `norm_index`.
- **Output**: Returns the normalized index as an unsigned 32-bit integer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_number_is_infinity`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_infinity>)
    - [`ecma_number_to_uint32`](<../base/ecma-helpers-conversion.c.md#ecma_number_to_uint32>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_string\_prototype\_object\_index\_of<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_string_prototype_object_index_of}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L484>)

Finds the index of a search string within a given string, starting from a specified position, and returns the index or -1 if not found.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The string in which to search for the substring.
    - ``arg1``: The substring to search for within `this_arg`.
    - ``arg2``: The position in `this_arg` from which to start the search.
    - ``first_index``: A boolean indicating whether to find the first or last occurrence of the substring.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Check if `this_arg` is coercible to an object; if not, return an error.
    - Convert `this_arg` to a string and store it in `to_str_val`; if conversion fails, return an error.
    - Convert `arg1` to a string and store it in `search_str_val`; if conversion fails, return an error.
    - Convert `arg2` to a number and store it in `pos_num`; if conversion fails, return an error.
    - Get the length of the original string from `to_str_val`.
    - Normalize the starting index using `pos_num`, the length of the original string, and `first_index`.
    - Find the index of `search_str_val` in the original string starting from the normalized index; if found, set `ret_num` to the index, otherwise set it to -1.
    - Finalize all temporary values and return `ret_value` as a number.
- **Output**: Returns the index of the search string if found, or -1 if not found, as an `ecma_value_t` number.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_op_check_object_coercible`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_check_object_coercible>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_string`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_string>)
    - [`ecma_get_string_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_string_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_string_get_length`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_string_get_length>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_helper_string_index_normalize`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_string_index_normalize>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_helper_string_find_index`](<#ecma_builtin_helper_string_find_index>)
    - [`ecma_make_number_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_number_value>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_string\_find\_index<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_string_find_index}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L558>)

Finds the index of a search string within an original string, either from the start or end, based on the specified parameters.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``original_str_p``: Pointer to the original string where the search is performed.
    - ``search_str_p``: Pointer to the string to search for within the original string.
    - ``first_index``: Boolean indicating whether to search for the first (true) or last (false) occurrence of the search string.
    - ``start_pos``: Starting position in the original string from where the search begins.
    - ``ret_index_p``: Pointer to store the position found in the original string if a match is found.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `match_found` to false and get the lengths of the original and search strings.
    - Check if the search string length is less than or equal to the original string length.
    - If the search string is empty, set `match_found` to true and `ret_index_p` to 0 or the original string length based on `first_index`.
    - Convert the original and search strings to UTF-8 and set the initial search position.
    - Iterate through the original string starting from `start_pos`, attempting to match the search string at each position.
    - If a match is found, set `match_found` to true, store the index in `ret_index_p`, and break the loop.
    - If no match is found, adjust the search position based on `first_index` and continue searching.
    - Finalize the UTF-8 strings and return `match_found`.
- **Output**: Returns a boolean indicating whether a match was found, and if so, updates `ret_index_p` with the index of the match.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_string_get_length`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_string_get_length>)
    - [`lit_utf8_incr`](<../../lit/lit-strings.c.md#lit_utf8_incr>)
    - [`lit_utf8_read_next`](<../../lit/lit-strings.c.md#lit_utf8_read_next>)
    - [`lit_utf8_decr`](<../../lit/lit-strings.c.md#lit_utf8_decr>)

### ecma\_builtin\_helper\_def\_prop<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_helper_def_prop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-helpers.c#L668>)

Defines a property on an object with specified attributes.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``obj_p``: Pointer to the object on which to define the property.
    - ``index_p``: Pointer to the string representing the property name.
    - ``value``: Value to assign to the property.
    - ``writable``: Boolean indicating if the property is writable.
    - ``enumerable``: Boolean indicating if the property is enumerable.
    - ``configurable``: Boolean indicating if the property is configurable.
    - ``is_throw``: Boolean indicating if an error should be thrown on failure.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create an empty property descriptor using [`ecma_make_empty_property_descriptor`](<../base/ecma-helpers.c.md#ecma_make_empty_property_descriptor>).
    - Set `is_value_defined` to true and assign `value` to the property descriptor.
    - Set `is_writable_defined` to true and assign `writable` to the property descriptor.
    - Set `is_enumerable_defined` to true and assign `enumerable` to the property descriptor.
    - Set `is_configurable_defined` to true and assign `configurable` to the property descriptor.
    - Call [`ecma_op_object_define_own_property`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_define_own_property>) with the object, property name, property descriptor, and `is_throw` to define the property.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` indicating the result of the property definition operation.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_empty_property_descriptor`](<../base/ecma-helpers.c.md#ecma_make_empty_property_descriptor>)
    - [`ecma_op_object_define_own_property`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_op_object_define_own_property>)
